data.title: Reflex Advanced Materials announces shares for debt settlement
data.description:
• Reflex Advanced Materials (RFLXF) said on Tuesday it has entered into a debt settlement agreement with a service provider of the company.
  • Under the agreement, the company will settle $45,000 of debt through the issuance of 246,981 common shares at a deemed price of $0.1822 per share.
  • The agreement and related share issuance are subject to CSE approval, with the securities subject to a four-month-and-one-day hold period.
  • Shares +7.38%.

data.title: Musk says AI will lead to universal abundance and saving for retirement will be irrelevant
data.description:
Billionaire businessman Elon Musk last week called artificial intelligence and robotics a "supersonic tsunami" that will lead to abundance for all humans, making saving for retirement irrelevant.

Musk's remarks came in an appearance on Peter Diamandis' podcast on YouTube last Tuesday. He has long been an advocate of AI and robotics and has made several public comments supporting the technology. On the podcast, Musk said he is "more optimistic than most people" about the effects of AI.

"Don't worry about squirreling money away for retirement in 10 or 20 years. It won't matter," Musk said. He believes AI will lead to people having a "universal you-can-have-whatever-you-want income." 

"My best guess for how this will manifest is that prices will...drop. So as the efficiency of production or the provision of services drops, prices will drop," the billionaire said. 

"I mean prices in dollar terms are the ratio between the output of goods and services and the money supply. So if your output of goods and services increases faster than the money supply, you will have deflation, or vice versa," he added.

Musk also painted a picture of a future where AI would have outstripped human intellect and people would be outnumbered by robots.

"There's going to be more intelligence, digital intelligence, than all human intelligence combined and more humanoid robots than all humans," he said.

"The white-collar laborer will be the first to go because until you can move atoms, the thing that can be replaced first is anything that involves just digital," Musk noted.

"Even with AI at its current state, I'd say you're pretty close to being able to replace half of all jobs, of white-collar jobs; that includes anything like education, too. Anything that involves information. And anything short of shaping atoms, AI can do probably half or more of those jobs right now," the businessman added.

Musk, the world's richest person, is the CEO of electric vehicle maker Tesla (TSLA), space exploration and aerospace firm SpaceX (SPACE), founder of startup xAI (X.AI) through which he owns X (formerly Twitter), and leader of Neuralink and The Boring Company.

Seeking Alpha's trending AI stocks: (PATH), (DELL), (AMBA), (AMD), (BBAI), (ADSK), (III), (TWLO), (ANET), (KVYO), (FRSH), (APP), (INTA), (OS), (API), and (WDH).

Here are some AI- and robotics-focused exchange-traded funds: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), and (CHAT).

data.title: AirJoule Technologies announces proposed public offering of class A common stock
data.description:
• AirJoule Technologies (AIRJ) on Tuesday announced the commencement of an underwritten public offering of shares of its Class A common stock, par value $0.0001 per share.
  • The company said Patrick Eilers, Executive Chairman and member of Board of Directors, CEO Matthew Jore, and Stuart Porter, a member of Board of Directors, have indicated an interest in purchasing shares of Class A common stock in this offering at the public offering price per share and on the same terms as the other purchasers in this offering. 
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds it receives from the offering to fund growth capital, working capital and for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mid to low cap materials stocks with the strongest earnings momentum
data.description:
As the earnings season kicks off, we look at mid-to-low market cap materials stocks with strong earnings momentum, which often attract investor attention as positive analyst revisions to earnings estimates can signal improving fundamentals.

The EPS Revision rating evaluates how analysts have adjusted their earnings per share estimates for a company over recent periods, with upgrades suggesting growing confidence in a company’s profitability outlook.

The following low to mid cap materials sector stocks have earned the highest possible EPS revision rating of A+, indicating the most favorable trajectory of analyst earnings estimate changes.

The following materials stocks have earned an A+ EPS revision grade:

  1. Constellium SE (CSTM) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  2. Flotek Industries (FTK) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  3. Fuchs SE (FUPBY) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  4. Warrior Met Coal (HCC) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  5. K+S Aktiengesellschaft (KPLUY) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  6. Nexa Resources S.A. (NEXA) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  7. Perimeter Solutions (PRM) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  8. Santacruz Silver Mining (SCZMF) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  9. Stora Enso Oyj (SEOAY) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  10. SunCoke Energy (SXC) — EPS Revision Grade: A+

 Materials ETFs: (XLB), (VAW), (IYM), (FXZ), (MXI), (RSPM)

data.title: Copa Holdings reports 10.9% rise in December passenger traffic
data.description:
• Copa Holdings (CPA) on Tuesday said its system-wide passenger traffic, measured in revenue passenger miles (RPMs), rose 10.9% in December 2025 from a year earlier.
  • Capacity, measured in available seat miles (ASMs), increased 10.0% during the month, while the load factor improved to 86.0% from 85.3% in December 2024.
  • CPA -0.0% after hours to $130.05.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Guardian Pharmacy expects 2026 revenue between $1.40B and $1.42B
data.description:
• Guardian Pharmacy Services (GRDN) on Tuesday said it expects revenue in the range of $1.40 billion to $1.42 billion for 2026, after the effects of the drug-pricing reforms under the Inflation Reduction Act and the more recently announced wholesale acquisition cost reductions.
  • Excluding the impact of the mandatory drug price reductions, the company would have projected 2026 revenue growth in the high single digits.
  • The company said it expects to continue its low double-digit adjusted EBITDA growth trajectory in 2026, supported by the strength of its operating model and increased visibility into the year ahead, including anticipated drug pricing changes.
  • The company expects 2026 adjusted EBITDA in the range of $115 million to $118 million, representing approximately 11% year-over-year growth from 2025 guidance.
  • Guardian is reiterating its previously announced 2025 revenue guidance of $1.43 billion to $1.45 billion and adjusted EBITDA guidance of $104 million to $106 million. 
  • GRDN +1.98% after hours to $31.88.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Taseko completes Florence construction, reports higher H2 Gibraltar copper output
data.description:
Taseko Mines (TGB) closed +3.9% on Tuesday, reaching a multiyear high of $6.56, after saying  construction activity at its Florence copper project in Arizona is complete, with its focus now turning to wellfield operations and commissioning of the SX/EW plant.

Injection flowrates in the wellfield have met or exceeded expectations to-date, resulting in faster initial acidification of the wellfield, Taseko (TGB) said, and the grade of copper recovered in solution from the recovery wells has continued to increase, with the average solution grade reaching the level required for SX/EW plant operations, which are expected to begin soon.

At the Gibraltar mine in British Columbia, Taseko (TGB) reported 2025 copper production of 98M lbs and molybdenum production of 1.9M lbs, with full-year sales of copper and molybdenum totaliing 99M lbs and 1.9M lbs, respectively.

Q4 Gibraltar copper production totaled 31M lbs, a significant increase over previous quarters of 2025, with copper head grade increasing to 0.26%, in line with expectations, while molybdenum output in the quarter was 800K lbs, ~50% higher than Q3 and the best quarterly production in eight years.

"Gibraltar production in the second half of the year was a notable improvement over the first half of the year with higher grades and better quality ore. Looking ahead to 2026, we expect more consistent quarterly production, now that we are better situated in the Connector pit, and higher overall copper production," President and CEO Stuart McDonald said.

data.title: Cheche Group receives Nasdaq notice for non-compliance
data.description:
• Cheche Group (CCG)  announced on Tuesday that it received a notification letter from Nasdaq indicating that the company regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement rule of $1.00 per share for continued listing.
  • The company would like to clarify that the notification letter has no current effect on the listing or trading of the company's securities on Nasdaq. 
  • The company said it intends to monitor the closing bid price of its Class A ordinary shares between now and July 13, 2026, and is considering its options to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement under the Nasdaq Listing Rules. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Versant closes on the acquisition of Free TV Network
data.description:
Versant (VSNT) announced on Tuesday that it closed on the acquisition of Free TV Network.

The Atlanta‑based media company operates free, ad‑supported national TV networks delivered both over‑the‑air and as FAST streaming channels, with a focus on value‑conscious viewers in the U.S. Free TV Network operates across the core markets of political news and opinion, business news and personal finance, golf and athletics participation, and sports and genre entertainment. The markets are served through a portfolio of iconic and innovative brands, including CNBC, MS NOW, USA Network, Golf Channel, Oxygen, E!, SYFY, along with complementary digital assets including Fandango, Rotten Tomatoes, GolfNow, and GolfPass

Free TV Network will operate within Versant as a dedicated business to help expand the company’s reach across over-the-air and FAST distribution. Free TV Network's portfolio of multicast and FAST networks is seen as complementing Versant's (VSNT) existing portfolio of brands by introducing a distinct distribution model designed to serve growing segments of viewers who increasingly engage with free television options.

Versant (VSNT) is bringing in Free TV Network founder Jonathan Katz to lead the day-to-day operations. He will report to Versant (VSNT) Chief Revenue and Business Officer David Pietrycha.

Shares of Versant (VSNT) have picked up a little bit of momentum, with a 4.6% swing higher over the last week.
